referenced_section_hint: section 11AF
description: This Prudential Standard is made under section 11AF of the Banking Act 1959.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Measurement of Capital — deductions from CET1, Tier 1, Total Capital
description: Assets required to be deducted from CET1, Tier 1 or Total Capital under APS 111 are excluded from the scope of APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: Securitisation — securitisation exposures
description: Securitisation exposures subject to APS 120 are excluded from APS 112 scope, except for funding-only or synthetic securitisations where underlying exposures must be included in RWA calculations.

referenced_section_hint: Covered Bonds — liabilities of covered bond SPV
description: Liabilities of a covered bond special purpose vehicle to an issuing ADI as specified in APS 121 are excluded from APS 112 scope.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Market Risk — items subject to market risk capital requirements
description: Items subject to capital requirements under APS 116 that do not have counterparty credit risk exposure under APS 180 are excluded from APS 112 scope.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Counterparty Credit Risk
description: APS 180 is referenced for the definition of counterparty credit risk exposure and for items excluded from APS 112 scope.

referenced_section_hint: Definitions
description: Terms defined in APS 001 Definitions appear in bold on first use in this Prudential Standard.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment D, paragraph 41 — unrealised fair value losses on liquid assets
description: ADIs with liquid assets at amortised cost may adjust exposures for CET1 deductions to reflect unrealised fair value losses at portfolio level as allowed under paragraph 41 of Attachment D to APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: OTC and exchange-traded derivative transactions, long-settlement transactions, adjusted CEM
description: For significant financial institutions, EAD for OTC and exchange-traded derivative transactions or long-settlement transactions must be determined in accordance with APS 180.

referenced_section_hint: Credit Risk Management — non-performing exposure definition
description: Defaulted exposure is defined as a non-performing exposure as defined in APS 220 Credit Risk Management.

referenced_section_hint: Associations with Related Entities — related entity definition
description: Related-party exposure is defined by reference to APS 222 Associations with Related Entities.

referenced_section_hint: Liquidity — liquid assets
description: Liquid assets referenced in the RWA calculation for on-balance sheet exposures are defined by reference to APS 210 Liquidity.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment E — defaulted exposures
description: Defaulted property exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E to this Prudential Standard rather than Attachment A.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment C — undrawn committed amounts for LVR calculation
description: The loan amount for LVR purposes includes any undrawn committed amount as set out in Attachment C to this Prudential Standard.

referenced_section_hint: independent property valuation requirements
description: Property valuations must be appraised independently using prudently conservative criteria consistent with the requirements set out in APS 220.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment D – unsettled and failed transactions
description: Unsettled and failed transactions must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment D to APS 112 rather than Attachment B.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment E – defaulted exposures
description: Defaulted exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E to APS 112 rather than Attachment B.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment F – external credit rating requirements
description: Where Attachment B requires risk weights based on external credit ratings, ADIs must also comply with the requirements in Attachment F to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment A paragraphs 3 to 7 – standard loan criteria
description: Residential and commercial property exposures in covered bond cover pools must meet the standard loan requirements in paragraphs 3 to 7 of Attachment A to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: Table 7 – risk weights for bank exposures
description: Unrated or non-qualifying covered bonds must be treated as bank exposures and risk-weighted using Table 7 of Attachment B.

referenced_section_hint: paragraph 41 – exposures through a third party
description: Exposures through a third party as detailed in paragraph 41 must not be included as corporate exposures.

referenced_section_hint: Table 10 – risk weights for general corporate exposures
description: Rated specialised lending exposures with issue-specific external credit ratings must use Table 10 risk weights.

referenced_section_hint: deduction of subordinated debt from regulatory capital
description: Subordinated debt not required to be deducted from regulatory capital under APS 111 must be risk-weighted at 150%.

referenced_section_hint: deduction of equity exposures from Regulatory Capital; deduction threshold for banking or insurance subsidiaries at Level 1 in Attachment D
description: Equity exposures not required to be deducted from Regulatory Capital under APS 111 must be risk-weighted at 250% or 400%; the risk-weighting of equity in banking or insurance subsidiaries at Level 1 is subject to the deduction threshold in Attachment D to APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: Attachments A, B and D – risk-weighting of exposures
description: After applying CCFs, ADIs must risk-weight off-balance sheet exposures according to the requirements in Attachments A, B and D to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: risk weights based on counterparty type
description: ADIs applying counterparty-based risk weights for equity exposures must refer to Attachment B of APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: criteria for recognising guarantees for risk-weighting purposes
description: Where a rated entity has guaranteed another entity's exposure, the guarantee may be recognised for risk-weighting purposes if the criteria in Attachment I are satisfied.

referenced_section_hint: risk weights for counterparties including third-party banks and sovereigns
description: Attachment G references Attachment B for determining risk weights of third-party banks holding cash collateral and for identifying sovereign securities eligible for zero per cent risk weight.

referenced_section_hint: definition of senior securitisation exposures and resecuritisation exposures
description: Attachment G references APS 120 for the definitions of senior securitisation exposures (eligible as collateral with credit rating grade of one) and resecuritisation exposures (not eligible as collateral).

referenced_section_hint: definition of qualifying central counterparties and adjusted CEM for OTC derivatives
description: Attachment G references APS 180 for the definition of qualifying central counterparties (core market participants) and for the adjusted CEM methodology applicable to OTC derivatives in EAD calculations.

referenced_section_hint: criteria for credit derivatives applicable to cash-funded credit-linked notes
description: Cash-funded credit-linked notes issued by the ADI against banking book exposures may be treated as cash collateralised transactions if they fulfil the criteria for credit derivatives in Attachment J.

referenced_section_hint: eligible bilateral netting agreement requirements for SFTs
description: Paragraph 33 requires that SFTs covered by an eligible bilateral netting agreement must meet the requirements detailed in Attachment H to this Prudential Standard.

referenced_section_hint: simple or comprehensive approach for RWA calculation; eligible collateral criteria
description: Paragraphs 6 and 8 of Attachment H reference Attachment G for eligible collateral criteria and the simple or comprehensive approach for calculating RWA when netting loans and deposits.

referenced_section_hint: deduction of retained first loss position from Common Equity Tier 1 Capital
description: Paragraph 6 of Attachment I requires that a guarantee with a materiality threshold equivalent to a retained first loss position must be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 Capital in accordance with APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: securitisation exposure definition
description: Footnote 24 in Table 23 of Attachment G references APS 120 for the meaning of securitisation exposure used in the supervisory haircuts table.

referenced_section_hint: paragraph 32 — haircut adjustment for revaluation frequency
description: Paragraph 10 of Attachment I requires that the currency mismatch haircut for guarantees be adjusted in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G regarding revaluation frequency.

referenced_section_hint: synthetic securitisation requirements
description: Paragraph 4 of Attachment J requires that where an ADI buys credit protection through a credit derivative forming part of a synthetic securitisation, the requirements of APS 120 apply.

referenced_section_hint: cash-collateralised transactions
description: Paragraph 3 of Attachment J states that qualifying cash-funded credit linked notes may be treated as cash-collateralised transactions under Attachment G.

referenced_section_hint: CET1 deduction for materiality thresholds
description: Paragraph 13 of Attachment J requires that credit derivative materiality thresholds equivalent to retained first loss positions be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 Capital in accordance with APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: synthetic securitisation — tranched cover
description: Paragraph 15 of Attachment J requires that where partial credit derivative coverage involves a seniority difference between protected and unprotected portions, the arrangement is treated as a synthetic securitisation subject to APS 120.

referenced_section_hint: paragraph 32 — haircut adjustment for revaluation frequency
description: Paragraph 17 of Attachment J requires that the currency mismatch haircut for credit derivatives be adjusted in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G regarding revaluation frequency.
